compressionAudit.sharedInvariant: Every stored row in the 26-bucket post-post-post-successor boundary has exactly two roots at its first post-post-post-successor obstruction prime.
compressionAudit.nonTerminalReason: The shared two-root law emits rank children and a still larger q-avoiding boundary; it does not prove terminal closure of those children.
compressionAudit.noStrongerUniformTheoremThisTurn: The buckets mix previous post-post-successor, post-successor, successor, post-next, next-prime, and later-prime supports unevenly, so this packet records the exact finite partition and hands off a whole-boundary q-avoiding batch cover instead of claiming one closed terminal family.

proofBoundary: This packet accounts for every post-post-post-successor bucket emitted by the 24-bucket post-post-successor q-avoiding batch cover and turns the 26-bucket surface into an exact deterministic ranked boundary. It does not close the 545,790,988,054,702,573,768,204,062,331,322,316,786 post-post-post-successor root children, the 17,122,811,411,360,928,250,603,246,815,478,193,773,776,015 post-post-post-successor q-avoiding classes, the p479-available branch, the p479-unavailable complement, q97, p443-unavailable, the wider p4217 complement, collision-free matching, or Problem 848.

oneNextAction.action: Run a whole-boundary q-avoiding batch cover over all 26 post-post-post-successor buckets, or emit an exact survivor/rank boundary if the batch cover does not close.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: If no whole-boundary cover exists, write the deterministic survivor boundary grouped by post-post-post-successor prime and previous post-post-successor/post-successor/successor/post-next/next/later-prime support; do not open a singleton q-child first.
oneNextAction.completionRule: Every one of the 26 post-post-post-successor q-avoiding buckets is covered, structurally decomposed, or listed in a deterministic exact survivor boundary before singleton descent.

nextTheoremMove: Use the deterministic 26-bucket post-post-post-successor rank boundary to run one whole-boundary q-avoiding batch cover; singleton q157/q163/etc. descent remains blocked until a later packet consumes a smaller finite token.
